Title: Secretary of War's Strategic Thinkers Program (STP)
Awardee: ARIZONA STATE UNIVERSITY Scottsdale AZ 85257 USA
Description: The Department of War awarded contract (47QFHA26C0002) for the Secretary of War (SECWAR) Strategic Thinkers Program (STP) Recompete, which provides a master’s degree-granting program for mid-career military officers and government civilians. This 12-month Master Degree program is established to develop and produce strategic thinkers for assignment to Combatant Commander staffs, the Joint Staff, and Military Services’ Headquarters, in support of the Deputy Assistant Secretary of War (DASW) requirements. The Strategic Thinkers Program Base Period is being incrementally funded IAW DFARS 252.232-7007 for an initial amount of $10,123.74 under CLIN 0001. The Government plans to award the additional funds necessary to fully fund the base period within the next 5 to 7 business days due to minor system issues. The parties contemplate that the Government will allot funds to this contract in accordance with the following schedule: On execution of contract $10,123.74. On or about 4/28/2026, $148,332.26, additional funding will be provided to CLIN 0001.

Title: TWO-PHASE DESIGN/BUILD REPAIR BUILDING 322 CEREBRO LABORATORY, KIRTLAND AIR FORCE BASE, BERNALILLO COUNTY, NEW MEXICO
Awardee: QA ENGINEERING LLC Albuquerque NM 87108 USA
Description: The project consists of the design and repair of the CEREBRO Laboratory Building 322.The building is approximately 34,000 GSF and includes Light Lab areas, Lab support spaces, existing Women's, and Men's restrooms to be updated to meet current Architectural Barriers Act (ABA) Standards, Air Force Instruction, and Building Code requirements. Also included in the scope of work is the upgrade of existing electrical power supply to meet Laboratories needs and the installation of a 10 Ton capacity Overhead Crane in the southeast High Bay. The interior renovations (approximately 13,500 SF) include repairs to the flooring, wall finishes, exterior doors, air lock interior doors, ceiling, electrical receptacles, communication, furniture, plumbing fixtures, and casework. This design shall conform to criteria established in the Air Force Corporate Facilities Standards (AFCFS), the Installation Facilities Standards (IFS). The project shall comply with applicable DoD, Air Force, and base design standards, environmental considerations, and requirements.

Title: Santa Rosa Janitorial Services
Awardee: RYMARC CONSTRUCTION INC Albuquerque NM 87107 USA
Description: The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers (USACE), Albuquerque District, has awarded a firm-fixed-price* sole source contract to an 8(a) Small Business. In accordance with FAR 5.705(b), please note that this contract action was not awarded competitively. The rationale for this non-competitive action is that the requirement was directed as a sole source award pursuant to Section 8(a) of the Small Business Act, as amended (15 U.S.C. 637(a)), as implemented by Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) Subpart 19.8, and the current Partnership Agreement between the Small Business Administration (SBA) and the Department of Defense. This is a follow-on requirement to a previously awarded 8(a) contract supporting the USACE Santa Rosa Dam Project. The project site includes one Administration area, an Overlook Facility/Comfort Station, and two walking trails, each with its own parking area. The contractor will provide year-round janitorial services in accordance with the established cleaning schedule. The Government shall not exercise any supervision or control over the contract service providers performing these services. Such service providers shall be accountable solely to the prime contractor, who, in turn, remains fully responsible to the Government.

Title: Military OneSource Program - Request for Proposal (RFP) 47QFCA23R0053 Reprocurement Award Notice
Description: The contract (47QFCA26C0001) resulting from solicitation number 47QFCA23R0053 and the termination of Contract 47QFCA25C0002 was awarded on April 17, 2026, to Leidos, Inc. for $456,277,600. This competitively awarded contract to Cognosante, LLC is in support of the Military OneSource Program for military members and their families, similar to a commercial Employee Assistance Program (EAP). This program provides members of the Armed Forces and their families, about 4.7 million persons or “participants” at locations worldwide, with a broad array of information and referrals to both military and civilian resources as well as counseling services. Through one centralized source, the services provided by Military OneSource are available 24 hours a day, seven days a week (24/7), via the Internet, telephone (i.e., 800 number and collect calls), electronic mail (e-mail), postal mail, secure real-time messaging (i.e.,chat), Artificial Intelligence (AI) chatbot, and, if requested, face-to-face counseling. This is a dynamic environment encompassing comprehensive support systems related to military members and their families.

Title: Inspection, Maintenance and Repair of Hydraulic Spud System on Crane Barge
PopStreetAddress: 431 North Shore Drive, Fountain City, WI 54629
Awardee: GTS-MC LLC 8902 RAMBIL RD Eau Claire WI 54703 USA
Description: The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers is seeking a Contractor to perform a complete diagnostic inspection, maintenance and repairs on the spud system of the USACE Crane Barge Leonard. The CB Leonard was constructed in 1998 and is equipped with a 75-ton crane. The spud system uses hydraulic pumps and electric winches to lower and raise a pair of ?spuds?, each weighing 28,500 lbs, to and from the river bottom, to hold the barge in place against the current during crane operations. The Contractor will perform a full inspection of all system components, perform maintenance and repairs necessary to eliminate all system chattering and excessive vibration (notable in one spud), and replace both inoperable deck control stations with new, fully functional controls. The work will take place aboard the CB Leonard, at Fountain City Service Base (FCSB) or a location to be determined (TBD) along the Mississippi River within a 100-mile radius of FCSB. Work will take place Monday ? Thursday, 0700 ? 1730, except Federal Holidays. Work must be completed no later than 15 June 2026. This solicitation is 100% set-aside for small businesses under NAICS 811310, with a small business size standard of $12,500,000. Offerors must be fully registered in the System for Award Management (SAM.gov) to be eligible for contract award. Quoters are strongly encouraged to visit the barge and inspect the equipment prior to quoting.

Title: Environmental Compliance IDIQ Services at Joint Region Marianas (JRM), Guam
Description: Contract Number N4019226D5000, Environmental Compliance Indefinite Delivery Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ) Services at Joint Region Marianas (JRM), Guam under the Cognizance of the Naval Facilities Engineering Systems Command, Marianas has been awarded to Ayuda Companies. The Contract is an indefinite delivery indefinite quantity with a maximum aggregate amount of $35,000,000.00 (12-month base period, four 12-month option periods, and optional six-month extension of services). Contract award was made for Mobilization and Base Period Recurring Work (20 April 2026 – 19 April 2027), Contract Line Item Numbers (CLIN) 0001 and 0002. If all options are exercised, the final completion date is 19 April 2031. The Contractor shall perform environmental services for JRM to ensure their operations demonstrate and maintain compliance with all applicable Federal, U.S. territory, and local environmental statutes and regulations, and with Department of Defense (DoD) and Navy environmental policies, permits, instructions, and guidance. Environmental services shall include the following environmental compliance programs: clean air, safe drinking water, clean water, hazardous waste, pollution prevention, oil management, solid waste management, pesticide compliance, Emergency Planning and Community Right to Know Act (EPCRA), ozone depleting substances management, storage tank management, environmental quality assessment, National Environmental Protection Act (NEPA), Environmental Management system, environmental sampling and analysis, and overall environmental compliance oversight. The work shall be performed for JRM installations, Supported Components and Tenant Commands on the island of Guam, including Marine Corps Base Camp Blaz (MCBCB) and Andersen Air Force Base (AAFB). Work may be required at locations other than JRM or Guam to ensure that JRM environmental operations and facilities comply with applicable statutes, regulations, policies, permits, instructions, and guidance.
